box.content: The Modern Monolith Architecture.

list.text: Laravel 11: The minimalist PHP framework.
Inertia.js: The bridge to modern SPAs.

box.content: Aravind Govindhasamy

list.text: Elite Technical Architect
PHP & Laravel Expert

box.content: Exploring the streamlined Laravel 11 core.
Laravel 11 reduces boilerplate by moving configuration out of the application and into the framework core.

list.text: Less Code: Fewer files in the default installation.
Streamlined: Simplified service providers and middleware.

box.content: A cleaner organization for modern PHP applications.

list.text: bootstrap/app.php: The new hub for routing and middleware.
config/: Fully optional - default values are now integrated.

box.content: Laravel 11 now uses SQLite as the default database, making local development even faster.

list.text: Zero Config: Start building immediately.
Fast: Perfect for rapid prototyping and testing.

box.content: How Inertia connects Laravel to modern frontend frameworks.
Inertia allows you to create fully client-side rendered, single-page apps, using classic server-side routing and controllers.

list.text: The Bridge: It sits between your server-side framework and your client-side framework.
No API Required: You don't need to build a REST or GraphQL API.

box.content: Inertia works by intercepting standard link clicks and sending an XHR request with specific headers.

list.text: Header-based: The server detects the header and returns JSON instead of HTML.
State Update: The client-side framework receives the JSON and updates the UI.

box.content: The first request returns full HTML with a root div, while subsequentnavigations return only data.

list.text: First Hit: Standard SSR for fast initial paint.
Navigation: Super fast JSON-only updates.

box.content: Defining routes and controllers for Inertia.
Use the same `routes/web.php` file you've always used.
Render your frontend components directly from your PHP controllers.
Passing dynamic data through URLs to your components.
Using the Ziggy package to access your Laravel routes directly in your Vue or React components.
Mastering Eloquent and global state sharing.
Transforming your database models into clean, predictable JSON for the frontend.
Sharing data (like the authenticated user) with every Inertia response automatically.
Optimize performance by only sending expensive data when it's explicitly requested by the frontend.
Securing your Inertia application.
A minimal, simple implementation of all of Laravel's authentication features, including login, registration, password reset, and dashboard.

list.text: Ready-to-go: Pre-configured with Inertia and Vue/React.
Tailwind: Styled with Tailwind CSS out of the box.

box.content: Providing a simple, uniform way to authorize user actions against your models.
Laravel sends authorization status to the frontend via shared data, allowing you to toggle UI elements securely.
Laravel's built-in protection against cross-site request forgery and other common attacks.

list.text: Auto-token: CSRF tokens are handled automatically by Inertia/Axios.
Helmet: Easy integration of security headers.

box.content: Mastering Vite in the Laravel ecosystem.
Vite provides a lightning-fast development experience with hot module replacement.
Inertia uses Vite to bundle your components for server-side rendering.

list.text: Unified: Same build process for client and server.
Performance: Fast initial paints with pre-rendered HTML.

box.content: Production strategies for modern PHP.
Painless server management and deployment optimized specifically for Laravel.

list.text: Infrastructure: Automate server setup (Nginx, MySQL, PHP).
CI/CD: Deploy on push to your main branch.

box.content: The serverless deployment platform for Laravel, powered by AWS Lambda.

list.text: Infinite Scale: Scale your app to millions of requests.
Cost Effective: Pay only for what you use.

box.content: Using Laravel Sail for local development and optimized Dockerfiles for production.

list.text: Sail: A lightweight CLI for interacting with Laravel's default Docker development environment.
Production: Multi-stage builds for lean PHP images.

box.content: Mastering persistent and nested layouts in Inertia.
Keep state (like playing audio or form input) as you navigate between pages.
Stacking layouts for deep application structures.
Detecting when a navigation is occurring to show loading states or progress bars.
The Inertia form helper and validation flow.
A powerful hook for managing form state, submission, and validation errors.
box.code:
const form = useForm({ email: '', password: '' });
form.post('/login');

box.content: Laravel handles the validation, and Inertia automatically shares the error bag with your component.
Easily toggle button states and show success messages using form helper properties.
Adding real-time interactivity with Laravel Reverb.
A first-party, high-performance WebSocket server specifically designed for Laravel.

list.text: Fast: Built for high throughput and low latency.
Integrated: Deeply integrated with Laravel Echo.

box.content: Trigger real-time updates from your PHP backend by implementing the ShouldBroadcast interface.
Using Laravel Echo to listen for events and update your Inertia state.
Testing Laravel and Inertia with Pest.
A delightful PHP testing framework with a focus on simplicity and readability.
Asserting that the correct components and data are being returned from your controllers.
Ensuring the full user flow works correctly in the browser.

list.text: Cross-browser: Test across different rendering engines.
Robust: Handle asynchronous interactions with ease.

box.content: The Future of Full-stack Laravel.
The most productive and mature way to build powerful, modern web applications.

list.text: Velocity: Move from idea to production faster than any other stack.
Stability: A mature ecosystem with long-term support.
Experience: A delightful developer experience on both backend and frontend.

box.content: Stay updated with the latest in the Laravel ecosystem.

list.text: Growth: Laravel continues to innovate at a rapid pace.
Tooling: New first-party tools are released regularly.

box.content: Start your Laravel 11 journey today.

list.text: Build: Install Laravel with the Inertia starter kit.
Learn: Explore Laracasts and the official documentation.

box.content: Thank you for your time! Any questions?
